com.newland.nsdk.core.api.internal.printer

Interface Printer

  • All Superinterfaces:
    Module

    public interface Printer
extends Module
    Provides the ability to print.

    How to get this module:

         Printer printer = (Printer)NSDKModuleManagerImpl.getInstance().getModule(ModuleType.PRINTER);

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void cutPaper()
      Cuts paper.(Only supported on CPOS X5 device)
      void feedPaper()
      Feeds paper.
      PrinterStatus getStatus()
      Gets printer status.
      void print(java.lang.String[] printContent, java.lang.String[] saveContent, java.lang.String path)
      Prints content and save the printed content to the target path.
      void printImage(byte[] image, int startX, int imageWidth, int imageHeight, PrintingResultListener printingResultListener)
      Prints an image with its actual width and height.
      void printRGBAImage(byte[] image, PrintingParameters parameters, PrintingResultListener printingResultListener)
      Prints an image with expected width and height.
      void setFont(java.lang.String fontPath)
      Sets the custom font for the follow-up printing procedure.
      void setGray(int gray)
      Sets printing gray.
      void setParams(PrnParams params)
      Sets the printing params
      boolean verifyLast()
      Compares the hash value of the save txt and the last content's, if it matches, this method will return true, which means the consistency of the content after power shut down occasionally.

    • Method Detail

      • printImage

        void printImage(byte[] image,
                int startX,
                int imageWidth,
                int imageHeight,
                PrintingResultListener printingResultListener)
         throws NSDKException
        Prints an image with its actual width and height.

        Note: Please make sure card reader is closed before printing. Otherwise it will fail to print.

        Example:

             // Get the bitmap according to your needs.
     Bitmap bitmap = getBitmap();

     int width = bitmap.getWidth();
     int height = bitmap.getHeight();

     ByteArrayOutputStream baos = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
     bitmap.compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.PNG, 100, baos);

     int startX = 0;

     PrintingResultListener listener = new PrintingResultListener() {
          @Override
          public void onEventRaised(int status) {
              // Handle the result.
          }
     };

     try{
         printer.printImage(baos.toByteArray(), startX, width, height, listener);
     } catch(NSDKException e){
         // Handle the exception.
     }
        Parameters:
        image - [Required] Compressed data of bitmap.
        startX - [Required] Starting Offset from where to print, shall be >=0.
        imageWidth - [Required] Width of the image, shall be >0.
        imageHeight - [Required] Height of the image, shall be >0.
        printingResultListener - [Required] Listens to the status of printer. See PrintingResultListener.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • printRGBAImage

        void printRGBAImage(byte[] image,
                    PrintingParameters parameters,
                    PrintingResultListener printingResultListener)
             throws NSDKException
        Prints an image with expected width and height.

        Note: Please make sure card reader is closed before printing. Otherwise it will fail to print.

        Example:

             // Get the bitmap according to your needs.
     Bitmap bitmap = getBitmap();

     PrintingParameters parameters = new PrintingParameters();
     parameters.setStartX(0);
     parameters.setImageWidth(bitmap.getWidth());
     parameters.setImageHeight(bitmap.getHeight());
     parameters.setExpectedImageWidth(bitmap.getWidth()/2);
     parameters.setExpectedImageHeight(bitmap.getHeight()/2);

     int size = bitmap.getByteCount();
     ByteBuffer buf = ByteBuffer.allocate(size);
     bitmap.copyPixelsToBuffer(buf);
     byte[] rgba = buf.array();

     PrintingResultListener listener = new PrintingResultListener() {
          @Override
          public void onEventRaised(int status) {
              // Handle the result.
          }
     };

     try{
         printer.printImage(rgba, parameters, listener);
     } catch(NSDKException e){
         // Handle the exception.
     }
        Parameters:
        image - [Required] RGBA data.
        parameters - [Required] Printing parameters, see PrintingParameters.
        printingResultListener - [Required] Listens to the status of printer. See PrintingResultListener.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • getStatus

        PrinterStatus getStatus()
                 throws NSDKException
        Gets printer status.
        Returns:
        See PrinterStatus
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • cutPaper

        void cutPaper()
       throws NSDKException
        Cuts paper.(Only supported on CPOS X5 device)
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • setGray

        void setGray(int gray)
      throws NSDKException
        Sets printing gray.
        Parameters:
        gray - [Required] Printing gray. Value range: [1,10].
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • setParams

        void setParams(PrnParams params)
        throws NSDKException
        Sets the printing params

        Note: This can be used to set whether to enable high-quality mode and set its gray.

        Parameters:
        params - [Required] Printing parameters to be set, details see PrnParams.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • feedPaper

        void feedPaper()
        throws NSDKException
        Feeds paper.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • print

        void print(java.lang.String[] printContent,
           java.lang.String[] saveContent,
           java.lang.String path)
    throws NSDKException
        Prints content and save the printed content to the target path.

        Note: This method can be only used in N950S-ECR devices, other devices call this will return "Unsupported"(-9999)

        Parameters:
        printContent - [Required] The script content for TTF-print.
        saveContent - [Required] The actual print content.
        path - [Required] The target txt file path which is used to save the actual printed content.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • verifyLast

        boolean verifyLast()
            throws NSDKException
        Compares the hash value of the save txt and the last content's, if it matches, this method will return true, which means the consistency of the content after power shut down occasionally.

        Note: This method can be only used in N950S-ECR devices, other devices call this will return "Unsupported"(-9999)

        Returns:
        The consistency of the content after the power shut down occasionally.
        Throws:
        NSDKException

      • setFont

        void setFont(java.lang.String fontPath)
      throws NSDKException
        Sets the custom font for the follow-up printing procedure.

        Note: This method can be only used in N950S-ECR devices, other devices call this will return "Unsupported"(-9999)

        Parameters:
        fontPath - [Required] The custom font path, which shall be in the "sys/fonts" path.
        Throws:
        NSDKException
